steps.title: Solve for v^2
steps.content: Start with 47 = \frac{2K}{v^2}. Multiply both sides by v^2: 47 v^2 = 2K. Divide both sides by 47: v^2 = \frac{2K}{47}.

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: v = \sqrt{\frac{2K}{47}}. Matches exactly. Works.
  • Choice B: v = \sqrt{\frac{47}{2K}} has the fraction inverted. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: v = \frac{47^2}{2K} is not a square root and the form is wrong. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: v = \frac{2K}{47^2} is not a square root and uses 47^2. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
